provider.ts.purpose: Provider Service, model catalog, SDK resolution, default model selection

transform.ts.purpose: Message transformation, temperature/topP/topK normalization, caching, reasoning conversion

auth.ts.purpose: Provider OAuth authorization flow via plugin hooks

error.ts.purpose: Provider-specific error classes, overflow detection (18 regex patterns)

bundled_sdks.note: Lazy-loaded via dynamic imports, LRU-cached (max 256 entries). SDKs and model instances cached separately.

custom_loaders.note: 700 lines of loader logic in custom() function. Handles auth, config injection, model discovery.

streaming.architecture: Dual-path: AI SDK v5 adapter + native runtime (OpenAI/Anthropic only)
streaming.timeouts: Header timeout (default 10s) + SSE chunk timeout via wrapSSE() + signal aggregation via AbortSignal.any()

code_smells.issue: 1916-line monolith with parallel registries between BUNDLED_PROVIDERS and transform.sdkKey()
process.env bypass in amazon-bedrock, google-vertex loaders
toPublicInfo() secret stripping is blacklist-based (apiKey, API_KEY only)

index.ts.purpose: Plugin Service, lifecycle, hooks, health/quarantine, auth registration

shared.ts.purpose: Plugin ID resolution, npm parsing, semver checks, entrypoint discovery

capability/enforcer.ts.purpose: checkCapability() — quarantine check, trust-level check, manifest check

capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts.purpose: 15 network-gated hooks require additional network.request capability

openai/codex.ts.purpose: OpenAI Codex OAuth PKCE auth plugin

openai/ws-pool.ts.purpose: WebSocket session pool with connection reuse, idle timeout, retry budget

stages: 2. RESOLUTION — Install → entrypoint discovery → compatibility check (retry on failure)
4. ACTIVATE — Register with capability registry → call config hook → subscribe to event bus
5. RUNTIME — Hook dispatch with capability gating, crash tracking, quarantine
6. DISPOSE — dispose hook on all plugins, health store saved
lifecycle.missing: No explicit deactivate/reload per plugin. No per-plugin lifecycle isolation.

trust_levels.built-in: All capabilities auto-granted (9 internal auth plugins)
trust_levels.verified: Defined but not used in runtime — no verification mechanism exists
trust_levels.external: Capability checks enforced. Default fallback grants only tool.register

broken: experimental.text.complete (network-gated but missing from HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP → always denied)

critical_gaps.issue: network.request capability NOT enforced at runtime — no fetch proxy
critical_gaps.description: The capability is defined and checked for network-gated hooks, but there is no fetch/HTTP proxy. Any plugin can call fetch() directly to exfiltrate data, bypassing the capability system entirely. Explicitly acknowledged in design docs.

critical_gaps.issue: filesystem.read and filesystem.write capabilities entirely unenforced
critical_gaps.description: Defined in types.ts but NEVER checked anywhere in the codebase. No entries in HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP. Plugins have unrestricted filesystem access through Node.js built-ins.

critical_gaps.issue: No OS-level sandboxing — plugins run in-process with full Node.js API access
critical_gaps.description: A malicious plugin can access fs, fetch, require, child_process, process.env directly. Capability system is check-box security that assumes cooperative plugins.

critical_gaps.issue: TOCTOU race in trigger() — snapshot of registrations captured but capability check uses live Ref state
critical_gaps.description: Between the shouldDispatch check and the hook call, another effect could mutate plugin security state.

critical_gaps.issue: Stale hook closures in event bus subscriber
critical_gaps.description: Event subscriber created at init time captures hooks via closure. If hooks are updated (re-init), old subscriber fires stale hooks.
It's listed in NETWORK_GATED_HOOKS but absent from HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP. shouldDispatch returns false for unknown hooks.

critical_gaps.issue: any type casts throughout capability system
critical_gaps.description: checkCapability(registry, pluginId, 'secrets.access' as any), double unsafe casts via (s.dispatchGuard as HookDispatchGuard).shouldDispatch(...) as any

tool.ts.purpose: Core Tool.Def, Context, error classes, Tool.define() factory

registry.ts.purpose: ToolRegistry Service — 60+ tool imports, plugin/MCP integration, schema conversion

cache.ts.purpose: Tool cache — @deprecated, not wired to any consumer

coordination.ts.purpose: Cross-session coordination: claims, reservations, fan-out, 8 wave types

dual_system.description: Two parallel tool systems coexist with different safety guarantees
dual_system.primary: Effect-based, permission-gated, file-locked, truncation, typed results
dual_system.smart_: spawnSync-based, naive substring filtering, NO permissions, NO truncation, NO locks, NO typed results

execution_pipeline: 1. ASSEMBLY — ToolRegistry.resolveTools() → filter by agent permission
3. CONVERSION — Effect Schema → JSONSchema7 → AI SDK tool()
4. STREAM — processor detects tool-call → resolve Tool.Def → fork execution
5. EXECUTION — permission check → external dir check → path reservation → file lock → core logic → post-write diagnostics
6. POST — TypedResult.wrap() → Truncate.output() → publish Bus events
7. DELIVERY — tool-result stream part → LLM next turn

critical_findings.issue: smart_bash shell injection — spawnSync(command, [], { shell: true }) with naive substring blocking
smart_bun, smart_grep, smart_find, smart_git also use spawnSync with shell-style construction — injection risk
critical_findings.note: Any tool constructing command strings passed to shell execution is vulnerable

critical_findings.issue: search_replace & replace_symbol bypass FileLock and coordination checks
critical_findings.note: Modify files with fewer guards than write/edit

critical_findings.issue: Placeholder tools (github_pr_search, github_triage) in production registry

index.ts.purpose: MCP Service — connection, tool discovery, conversion, OAuth

security_findings.issue: No MCP tool_use_id response verification (concurrent call confusion)
Stdio process PID tracked but no cleanup on abnormal exit (GC-dependent)
OAuth token storage — encryption status unclear

5_permission_model.bash_arity: ~150 command prefixes mapped to token counts for permission extraction
5_permission_model.flow: Tool.ask() → evaluate rules → auto-allow if matched → else prompt user → store rule in SQLite → proceed/reject

6_cross_cutting_findings.architecture: Effect v4 services with Layer.provide() DI. Bootstrap runtime wires all layers.
top_10_risks_ranked: 1. [CRITICAL] No plugin sandboxing — in-process execution with full Node.js API (fetch, fs, child_process, process.env)
2. [CRITICAL] network.request capability not enforced — no fetch proxy, any plugin can exfiltrate data
3. [CRITICAL] filesystem.read/write capabilities entirely unenforced — never checked anywhere
4. [CRITICAL] smart_bash shell injection via spawnSync(shell:true) with naive substring filtering
5. [HIGH] Dual tool system (primary vs smart_) with inconsistent safety guarantees — LLMs can call either
6. [HIGH] Placeholder tools (github_pr_search, github_triage) in production registry
7. [HIGH] search_replace & replace_symbol bypass FileLock and coordination checks
8. [HIGH] TOCTOU race in plugin trigger() — snapshot vs live Ref mismatch
10. [HIGH] experimental.text.complete hook always denied (capability mapping bug)
